changes.detail: The prompt lists backend/core/scoring/primitives.py as a 'known likely file to inspect'. THIS FILE DOES NOT EXIST. The actual primitives are in backend/core/analytics/primitives.py — confirmed by rules.py lines 12–17 which import position_in_range, position_in_one_sided_lab_range, map_position_to_status, and coerce_optional_float from core.analytics.primitives. Cursor must inspect backend/core/analytics/primitives.py, NOT backend/core/scoring/primitives.py, and must NOT create a new primitives.py under backend/core/scoring/.

changes.check: Stage 2A — ALT bypass confirmed in rules.py
changes.citation: backend/core/scoring/rules.py lines 309–311: hardcoded bypass 'if biomarker_name == "alt" and value < float(min_val): score, score_range = self._calculate_score_from_has_position(0.05); return score, score_range, None'. Comment attributes this to LC-S11A. This is the specific exception the sprint is governed to replace with a directionality policy. Existence and location confirmed.

changes.check: Stage 2A — Directionality field absent from scoring_policy.yaml
changes.citation: backend/ssot/scoring_policy.yaml read in full (349 lines). No 'direction_class', 'directionality', or equivalent field present anywhere in the file. Policy is entirely symmetric: position_in_range() maps value within [lab_min, lab_max] to 0..1; all below-range and above-range deviations are scored symmetrically by low_noncritical/low_critical and high_noncritical/high_critical curves. This confirms the scaffold gap the sprint is addressing.

changes.check: Stage 2A — SOURCE CROSS-REF: GGT, ALP, ApoA1 absent from scoring_policy.yaml — POLICY EXPANSION REQUIRED
changes.detail: The prompt requires directionality coverage for GGT, ALP, ApoA1 as representative markers. These biomarkers exist in backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml (ggt at line 1423, alp at line 1443, apoa1 at line 159) but are NOT present in backend/ssot/scoring_policy.yaml: (1) the liver system only contains 'alt' and 'ast' — GGT and ALP are absent; (2) the cardiovascular system does not include ApoA1. Directionality policy cannot be applied to markers that are not in the scoring policy. Cursor must therefore add GGT, ALP, and ApoA1 entries (with directionality) to scoring_policy.yaml as part of this sprint. This is within the 'Potentially allowed' list (scoring_policy.yaml, biomarkers.yaml). Cursor must document this policy expansion in the LC-S14 notes file. If Cursor cannot establish consensus policy bands for any of these markers, it must mark them as deferred per prompt §Phase 2 guidance.

changes.check: Stage 2A — scoring_policy.yaml HDL bands — asymmetry already present but not enforced
changes.citation: backend/ssot/scoring_policy.yaml lines 118–130: hdl_cholesterol bands are already structured with low values as concerning (critical: min 0.0, max 0.26) and high values as optimal (optimal: min 1.55, max 5.17). However, the symmetric position_in_range() used by rules.py will still penalise very high HDL (>5.17) identically to very low HDL. This confirms the 'protective-high' problem is real and must be resolved by the directionality mechanism.

sentinel/packs/ directory exists but is EMPTY — no JSON files present. The five required Sentinel defect classes (direction_sensitive_marker_false_alarm, low_enzyme_false_alarm, protective_high_marker_penalised, hardcoded_biomarker_scoring_exception, scoring_signal_directionality_conflation) must be CREATED from scratch, not updated. No existing pack files conflict.

Signal libraries explicitly excluded. Prohibition on signal activation changes is consistent with the sprint scope.

changes.detail: No competing LC-S14 prompt exists outside latest_cursor_prompt.md. No duplicate scoring authority source detected. scoring_policy.yaml is the sole SSOT for scoring policy. The 'Potentially allowed' list does not include creating a second scoring policy file.

changes.check: Stage 2B — Intelligence Core components in scope identified
changes.detail: Intelligence Core components affected: (1) backend/core/scoring/rules.py — primary target: remove ALT bypass at lines 309–311, add directionality dispatch; (2) backend/ssot/scoring_policy.yaml — add direction_class fields, add GGT/ALP/ApoA1 entries; (3) backend/core/scoring/engine.py — potential secondary impact if score aggregation needs to respond to directionality; (4) Sentinel packs — new defect class entries. All HIGH-risk. No other Intelligence Core components identified as in-scope.

changes.check: Stage 2B — Behavioural surface area and expected output changes
changes.detail: Scores that will intentionally change: (a) low ALT below lab lower bound — currently scores ~borderline (position 0.05 hardcoded); after sprint should be non-alarming per policy; (b) very high HDL/ApoA1 above lab upper bound — currently scored symmetrically as concerning; after sprint should be non-penalised or capped at neutral/informational per policy; (c) low AST/GGT/ALP — should become non-alarming per high-only policy class. Domain/system aggregate scores for liver and cardiovascular systems may shift accordingly for panels containing these edge cases. All changes are intentional and must be documented in the LC-S14 notes file.

Protected behaviours explicitly listed in prompt and verified as active test targets: LC-S8F/G unit/display fidelity, LC-S8D unit governance, LC-S10B launch-core protection, LC-S11A trust blockers, LC-S13 lifestyle/coherence/narrative. All test files present (confirmed above). ApoB/ApoA1 ratio interpretation is explicitly protected — ratio logic must remain separate from HDL/ApoA1 individual marker directionality. Homocysteine lead finding is explicitly protected. No test is listed for homocysteine lead specifically; Cursor should run the broader test suite if domain aggregation changes appear.

changes.check: Stage 2B — No scope creep into signal libraries or Knowledge Bus
changes.detail: Sprint correctly prohibits: signal activation changes, Knowledge Bus content, frontend rendering, narrative compilers, unit conversion, lifestyle propagation. Directionality is confined to scoring policy and the rules.py dispatch layer. Signal firing and scoring directionality are explicitly separated. Any finding that these cannot be separated requires STOP for GPT review (correctly specified in prompt Phase 3).

Directionality must be data-driven (policy-file-based), not hardcoded per marker. Missing directionality configuration must use safe default (symmetric) behaviour — not an arbitrary fallback that could drift. Unknown biomarker names must not cause exceptions. Policy file must reject invalid direction class values. All these determinism constraints are explicitly required by the prompt. No silent drift risk identified beyond the policy-expansion warning already flagged.

changes.check: Stage 2B — STOP condition: competing scoring authorities
changes.detail: Prompt correctly requires STOP if multiple competing scoring authorities are found and the correct one cannot be established. Verified: only one scoring authority source exists (scoring_policy.yaml loaded by core/analytics/scoring_policy_registry.py via load_scoring_policy()). No competing authority exists. STOP condition is not triggered.

changes.check: Stage 2B — No-op check: real work exists
changes.detail: Not a no-op sprint. Real defects exist: (1) hardcoded ALT bypass at rules.py:309–311; (2) no directionality framework anywhere in scoring_policy.yaml; (3) GGT/ALP/ApoA1 absent from scoring_policy.yaml despite being in biomarkers SSOT; (4) HDL protective-high not enforced by scoring despite band structure implying low concern. All four items require code and/or SSOT changes. Sprint is justified.

Five defect classes required: direction_sensitive_marker_false_alarm, low_enzyme_false_alarm, protective_high_marker_penalised, hardcoded_biomarker_scoring_exception, scoring_signal_directionality_conflation. Each must point to an active deterministic regression test. sentinel/packs/ is currently empty — all five classes are new entries. Placeholder entries are explicitly prohibited. Cursor must not add a class until the backing test exists and passes.

verdict: HARDENED. All five front matter fields valid. risk_level HIGH and change_type BEHAVIOUR correctly classified. All controlling authority docs present. All seven regression test files confirmed present. Two warnings recorded: (1) PATH DISCREPANCY — backend/core/scoring/primitives.py does not exist; real primitives are at backend/core/analytics/primitives.py; Cursor must NOT create a new file there; (2) POLICY EXPANSION REQUIRED — GGT, ALP, and ApoA1 do not exist in scoring_policy.yaml; they must be added with governed bands and directionality as part of this sprint; if bands cannot be established they must be explicitly deferred with reason. ALT bypass confirmed at rules.py:309–311. Directionality field confirmed absent from scoring_policy.yaml. Sentinel packs/ is empty — all five defect classes must be created, not updated. Cursor may proceed to Stage 3 kernel start after acknowledging both warnings.
